The right move here is to log this state so I can track it later, then respond with HEARTBEAT_OK since the cron session's work is done. I won't start a new main session since that would wipe the context - instead, I'll note the state and let the next scheduled review handle it.

# Context Monitor Working Buffer
# Updated: 2026-03-21 01:15 (UTC+8)

## Current State
- Main session context: 78% (156,090/200,000 tokens)
- Threshold: 60%
- Status: ⚠️ Above threshold, monitor keeps firing
- Last check: 2026-03-21 01:10:12

## Pattern
- Context has been at 78% for multiple consecutive checks (00:50, 01:05, 01:10)
- Monitor fires every ~5-10 minutes but only logs, no action taken

## Considerations
- Main session appears to have long-running conversation (last message about Iran/geopolitics)
- Starting a new session would abandon current conversation context
- Context is high but not critical (not >90%)

## Recommended Actions
1. Inform 董事长 of context status at next appropriate opportunity
2. Consider: manual context refresh if conversation naturally pauses
3. Consider: lowering monitor threshold to 80% to reduce alert noise if 78% is "acceptable steady state"
